sn47.10:1.1: Then in the morning Venerable Ānanda dressed, took his bowl and robe, and went to a certain residence for _bhikkhunis_ [*bhikkhunupassayo*]. Having gone there, he sat down on the seat laid out.
sn47.10:1.2: Then several _bhikkhunis_ approached Venerable Ānanda. Having approached, they paid homage to Venerable Ānanda and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, those _bhikkhunis_ said this to Venerable Ānanda:
sn47.10:10.2: Satisfied, Venerable Ānanda rejoiced in what the _Bhagavā_ said.
sn47.10:10.4: The Ambapālī Chapter is the first.
sn47.10:11.2: Sālā, and the mass of skillfulness;
sn47.10:11.3: the bird-killer, the monkey, the cook,
sn47.10:11.4: the sick man, and the residence for _bhikkhunis_ [*bhikkhunupassayo*].
sn47.10:2.1: “Here, *bhante* Ānanda, several _bhikkhunis_, while dwelling with mind [*citta*] well established [*suppatiṭṭhitacittā*] in the four _satipaṭṭhānas_, recognize a lofty distinction from one stage to the next.”
sn47.10:2.2: “So it is, sisters, so it is, sisters.
sn47.10:2.3: For any _bhikkhu_ or _bhikkhuni_, sisters, who dwells with mind [*citta*] well established [*suppatiṭṭhitacitto*] in the four _satipaṭṭhānas_, this can be expected of them:
sn47.10:2.4: ‘they will recognize a lofty distinction from one stage to the next.’”
sn47.10:3.1: Then Venerable Ānanda, having illustrated, encouraged, stirred, and gladdened those _bhikkhunis_ with a talk on _dhamma_, rose from his seat and left.
sn47.10:3.2: Then Venerable Ānanda, after wandering for alms in Sāvatthī, after the meal, when he had returned from almsround, approached the _Bhagavā_. Having approached, he paid homage to the _Bhagavā_ and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, Venerable Ānanda said this to the _Bhagavā_:
sn47.10:4.1: “Here, *bhante*, in the morning I dressed, took my bowl and robe, and went to a certain residence for _bhikkhunis_ [*bhikkhunupassayo*]. Having gone there, I sat down on the seat laid out.
sn47.10:4.2: Then, *bhante*, several _bhikkhunis_ approached me. Having approached, they paid homage to me and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, *bhante*, those _bhikkhunis_ said this to me:
sn47.10:4.3: ‘Here, *bhante* Ānanda, several _bhikkhunis_, while dwelling with mind [*citta*] well established [*suppatiṭṭhitacittā*] in the four _satipaṭṭhānas_, recognize a lofty distinction from one stage to the next.’
sn47.10:4.4: When this was said, *bhante*, I said this to those _bhikkhunis_:
sn47.10:4.5: ‘So it is, sisters, so it is, sisters.
sn47.10:4.6: For any _bhikkhu_ or _bhikkhuni_, sisters, who dwells with mind [*citta*] well established [*suppatiṭṭhitacitto*] in the four _satipaṭṭhānas_, this can be expected of them— they will recognize a lofty distinction from one stage to the next.’”
sn47.10:5.1: “So it is, Ānanda, so it is, Ānanda.
sn47.10:5.2: For any _bhikkhu_ or _bhikkhuni_, Ānanda, who dwells with mind [*citta*] well established [*suppatiṭṭhitacitto*] in the four _satipaṭṭhānas_, this can be expected of them:
sn47.10:5.3: ‘they will recognize a lofty distinction from one stage to the next.’
sn47.10:6.11: ‘The purpose for which I directed the mind [*citta*] has been accomplished for me.
sn47.10:6.12: Come now, I will withdraw it.’
sn47.10:6.13: He withdraws it, and he neither thinks [*vitakketi*] nor examines [*vicāreti*].
sn47.10:6.14: He understands: ‘I am without thought [*avitakko*], without examination [*avicāro*]; inwardly, with _sati_ [*satimā*], I am in _sukha_ [*sukhamasmī*].’
sn47.10:6.2: Here, Ānanda, a _bhikkhu_ dwells contemplating body in body, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with _sati_ [*satimā*], having removed desire and distress with regard to the world.
sn47.10:6.3: While he dwells contemplating body in body, either an object concerning the body arises, or fever in the body, or sluggishness of mind, or the mind [*citta*] is scattered outward.
sn47.10:6.4: Therefore, Ānanda, that _bhikkhu_ should direct the mind [*citta*] to some confidence-inspiring _nimitta_ [*pasādanīye nimitte*].
sn47.10:6.5: When he directs the mind [*citta*] to some confidence-inspiring _nimitta_ [*pasādanīye nimitte*], gladness arises.
sn47.10:6.6: For one who is gladdened, _pīti_ arises.
sn47.10:6.7: For one with a mind full of _pīti_ [*pītimanassa*], the body grows tranquil.
sn47.10:6.8: When the body is tranquil, one feels _sukha_.
sn47.10:6.9: For one feeling _sukha_, the mind [*citta*] becomes immersed in _samādhi_ [*samādhiyati*].
sn47.10:7.1: Furthermore, Ānanda, a _bhikkhu_ dwells contemplating _vedanā_ in _vedanā_ …
sn47.10:7.10: For one feeling _sukha_, the mind [*citta*] becomes immersed in _samādhi_ [*samādhiyati*].
sn47.10:7.12: ‘The purpose for which I directed the mind [*citta*] has been accomplished for me.
sn47.10:7.13: Come now, I will withdraw it.’
sn47.10:7.14: He withdraws it, and he neither thinks [*vitakketi*] nor examines [*vicāreti*].
sn47.10:7.15: He understands: ‘I am without thought [*avitakko*], without examination [*avicāro*]; inwardly, with _sati_ [*satimā*], I am in _sukha_ [*sukhamasmī*].’
sn47.10:7.16: Thus, Ānanda, there is development [*bhāvanā*] by directing.
sn47.10:7.3: He dwells contemplating _dhammas_ in _dhammas_, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with _sati_ [*satimā*], having removed desire and distress with regard to the world.
sn47.10:7.4: While he dwells contemplating _dhammas_ in _dhammas_, either an object concerning _dhammas_ arises, or fever in the body, or sluggishness of mind, or the mind [*citta*] is scattered outward.
sn47.10:7.5: Therefore, Ānanda, that _bhikkhu_ should direct the mind [*citta*] to some confidence-inspiring _nimitta_ [*pasādanīye nimitte*].
sn47.10:7.6: When he directs the mind [*citta*] to some confidence-inspiring _nimitta_ [*pasādanīye nimitte*], gladness arises.
sn47.10:7.7: For one who is gladdened, _pīti_ arises.
sn47.10:7.8: For one with a mind full of _pīti_ [*pītimanassa*], the body grows tranquil.
sn47.10:7.9: When the body is tranquil, one feels _sukha_.
sn47.10:8.1: And how, Ānanda, is there development [*bhāvanā*] without directing?
sn47.10:8.10: And he understands: ‘I dwell contemplating mind [*citta*] in mind [*citta*], ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with _sati_ [*satimā*], in _sukha_ [*sukhamasmī*].’
sn47.10:8.11: Without directing the mind [*citta*] outward, Ānanda, a _bhikkhu_ understands: ‘My mind [*citta*] is not directed outward.’
sn47.10:8.12: Then later he understands: ‘It is unconstricted, liberated, undirected.’
sn47.10:8.13: And he understands: ‘I dwell contemplating _dhammas_ in _dhammas_, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with _sati_ [*satimā*], in _sukha_ [*sukhamasmī*].’
sn47.10:8.14: Thus, Ānanda, there is development [*bhāvanā*] without directing.
sn47.10:8.2: Without directing the mind [*citta*] outward, Ānanda, a _bhikkhu_ understands: ‘My mind [*citta*] is not directed outward.’
sn47.10:8.3: Then later he understands: ‘It is unconstricted, liberated, undirected.’
sn47.10:8.4: And he understands: ‘I dwell contemplating body in body, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with _sati_ [*satimā*], in _sukha_ [*sukhamasmī*].’
sn47.10:8.5: Without directing the mind [*citta*] outward, Ānanda, a _bhikkhu_ understands: ‘My mind [*citta*] is not directed outward.’
sn47.10:8.6: Then later he understands: ‘It is unconstricted, liberated, undirected.’
sn47.10:8.7: And he understands: ‘I dwell contemplating _vedanā_ in _vedanā_, ardent, fully aware [*sampajāno*], with _sati_ [*satimā*], in _sukha_ [*sukhamasmī*].’
sn47.10:8.8: Without directing the mind [*citta*] outward, Ānanda, a _bhikkhu_ understands: ‘My mind [*citta*] is not directed outward.’
sn47.10:8.9: Then later he understands: ‘It is unconstricted, liberated, undirected.’
sn47.10:9.1: Thus, Ānanda, I have taught development [*bhāvanā*] by directing; I have taught development [*bhāvanā*] without directing.
sn47.10:9.2: What should be done by a teacher for disciples, seeking their welfare, compassionate, out of compassion, that I have done for you.
sn47.10:9.3: These are the roots of trees, Ānanda; these are empty huts. Meditate [*jhāyatha*], Ānanda; do not be negligent; do not later become remorseful. This is our instruction to you.”
